root_text.an3.76:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.76:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bhavo hotī”ti?
root_text.an3.76:2.1: “Kāmadhātuvepakkañca, ānanda, kammaṁ nābhavissa, api nu kho kāmabhavo paññāyethā”ti?
root_text.an3.76:2.3: “Iti kho, ānanda, kammaṁ khettaṁ, viññāṇaṁ bījaṁ, taṇhā sneho.
root_text.an3.76:2.4: Avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ hīnāya dhātuyā viññāṇaṁ patiṭṭhitaṁ evaṁ āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti hoti. (…)
root_text.an3.76:3.1: Rūpadhātuvepakkañca, ānanda, kammaṁ nābhavissa, api nu kho rūpabhavo paññāyethā”ti?
root_text.an3.76:3.3: “Iti kho, ānanda, kammaṁ khettaṁ, viññāṇaṁ bījaṁ, taṇhā sneho.
root_text.an3.76:3.4: Avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ majjhimāya dhātuyā viññāṇaṁ patiṭṭhitaṁ evaṁ āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti hoti. (…)
root_text.an3.76:4.1: Arūpadhātuvepakkañca, ānanda, kammaṁ nābhavissa, api nu kho arūpabhavo paññāyethā”ti?
root_text.an3.76:4.3: “Iti kho, ānanda, kammaṁ khettaṁ, viññāṇaṁ bījaṁ, taṇhā sneho.
root_text.an3.76:4.4: Avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ paṇītāya dhātuyā viññāṇaṁ patiṭṭhitaṁ evaṁ āyatiṁ punabbhavābhinibbatti hoti.

translation_text.an3.76:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.76:1.2: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘continued existence’.
translation_text.an3.76:2.1: “If, Ānanda, there were no deeds to result in the sensual realm, would continued existence in the sensual realm still come about?”
translation_text.an3.76:2.3: “So, Ānanda, deeds are the field, consciousness is the seed, and craving is the moisture.
translation_text.an3.76:2.4: The consciousness of sentient beings—shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving—is established in a lower realm. That’s how there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.an3.76:3.1: If there were no deeds to result in the realm of luminous form, would continued existence in the realm of luminous form still come about?”
translation_text.an3.76:3.3: “So, Ānanda, deeds are the field, consciousness is the seed, and craving is the moisture.
translation_text.an3.76:3.4: The consciousness of sentient beings—shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving—is established in a middle realm. That’s how there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.an3.76:4.1: If there were no deeds to result in the formless realm, would continued existence in the formless realm still come about?”
translation_text.an3.76:4.3: “So, Ānanda, deeds are the field, consciousness is the seed, and craving is the moisture.
translation_text.an3.76:4.4: The consciousness of sentient beings—shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving—is established in a higher realm. That’s how there is regeneration into a new state of existence in the future.
translation_text.an3.76:4.5: That’s how continued existence is defined.”

variant_text.an3.76:2.4: āyatiṁ → āyati (bj) | (…) → (evaṁ kho ānanda bhavo hotīti) (mr)
